summary.note: 1/5 success rate. Pattern: agent excels at acting (form-fill clicked + typed + submitted 7/8 steps perfectly), struggles to extract answers from SPA-rendered text in spans, code blocks, and slow-loading JS-heavy pages.

results.name: wv-arxiv-title
results.result: Title: Agent-E: From Autonomous Web Navigation to Foundational Design Principles in Agentic Systems

results.name: wv-github-stars
results.note: Star count visible at top, agent kept scrolling further. Star count rendered as generic role with text node, not as a labelled link/button — agent didn't recognize it as the answer.

results.name: wv-mdn-method
results.note: Bounced scroll up/down looking for Syntax section. Code blocks now in visible text but agent didn't find the right viewport.

results.name: wv-npm-version
results.note: npm.com is a slow JS SPA. Agent kept calling wait_for(3000ms). Version may have been visible after second wait but agent didn't snapshot-and-evaluate, it just kept waiting.

results.name: wv-form-fill
results.note: ACTING phase 7/8 perfect: clicked all 3 textboxes, typed each value, clicked Submit. Failed at READING the JSON response on httpbin's confirmation page. Single concrete fix: visible-text walker should include <pre> contents > 80 chars (currently capped).

knownGaps: Visible-text capture for JS-rendered SPAs (npm, GitHub) — fingerprint stability + re-read-after-wait would help.
Long <pre>/<code> blocks (httpbin JSON response) — current 800-char cap drops them.
Recognising 'the answer is already on screen' — agent often defaults to scroll instead of done.
